notes.note: A "free legation": a nominal, duty-free embassy that let a senator travel abroad on public business while drawing the privileges of an envoy. Here Decimus Brutus wants it as a respectable cover for leaving Italy.
The Roman formula of outlawry (aquae et ignis interdictio): a decree barring a man from fire and water within Italy, which amounted to a sentence of exile.

Sextus Pompeius (Pompey the Great's surviving son) held forces in Spain and the western seas, and Q. Caecilius Bassus held out for the Pompeian cause in Syria; Decimus names them as the only standing armies the assassins might fall back on. "This news about Caesar" is the report of the dictator's murder reaching the provinces.
